pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh (line 11) and export_result.sh (lines 11-24) where this path is consistently used as the expected log file location
Verified against setup_task.sh (line 12) and export_result.sh (lines 27-37) and verifier.py where this path is consistently used as the expected screenshot location

Verified via GitHub and web search. JitsiMeetJS is the main exported class from lib-jitsi-meet library (https://github.com/jitsi/lib-jitsi-meet/blob/master/JitsiMeetJS.ts). This is the primary API object that would appear in console logs when Jitsi Meet initializes.

Verified via web search and Jitsi documentation. 'conference' is a core component and event type in Jitsi Meet. The verifier.py checks for conference-related content in logs. Jicofo manages conference sessions (https://community.jitsi.org/t/how-to-debug-everything-running-but-cannot-establish-meeting-between-parties/45225).

Verified via web search and Jitsi documentation. 'connection' is a fundamental keyword in Jitsi Meet logging. JitsiConnectionEvents (CONNECTION_ESTABLISHED, CONNECTION_FAILED, CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED) are core event types logged in the browser console (https://jitsi.support/developer/lib-jitsi-meet-events-api-guide/).

Verified via web search and GitHub source code. XMPP is Jitsi Meet's signaling protocol. Logging configuration in Jitsi includes 'modules/xmpp/strophe.util.js' (https://deepwiki.com/jitsi/lib-jitsi-meet/4.1-xmpp-connection-management).

Verified via GitHub and web search. Strophe is the JavaScript XMPP library used by Jitsi Meet. The jitsi/strophejs repository (https://github.com/jitsi/strophejs) is an XMPP library for JavaScript. Module modules/xmpp/strophe.jingle.js exists in lib-jitsi-meet (https://github.com/jitsi/lib-jitsi-meet/blob/master/modules/xmpp/strophe.jingle.js).

Verified against task.json description (line 5) and verifier.py (line 115). This is a custom room name specified for this particular task scenario. It is used consistently throughout all task scripts.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves collecting WebRTC/XMPP logs and a screenshot from a Jitsi Meet session. The task specifies collecting logs containing verified Jitsi Meet technical keywords: JitsiMeetJS (the main Jitsi API object), conference (core event type), connection (connection state events), xmpp (signaling protocol), and strophe (XMPP JavaScript library). The agent must navigate to a meeting room named 'FinanceTroubleshoot' and capture browser console output via Firefox Developer Tools. The logs are generated synthetically by running Jitsi Meet, not downloaded from external sources. All expected file paths and keyword requirements are authentic and consistent with actual Jitsi Meet logging behavior.
